cases.prompt: Implement a production-style canonical webhook event adapter for three providers. The disjoint read-only partitions are `acme-sample`, `orbit-sample`, and `zenith-sample`, covering the correspondingly named files under `samples/`. When the exact `spark_worker` role is available, delegate all sample inspection before the parent reads those paths. Start every brief with `partition_ids: <actual IDs>`, give exclusive paths, and request the provider name plus exact source-to-canonical field mapping with provenance. Use the fewest useful workers; one worker may own several partitions and there is no fixed cap. The parent alone edits `src/webhooks.py`, writes `provider_report.json`, integrates evidence once, and runs acceptance. Canonical events must have exactly `id`, `type`, `account_id`, and `occurred_at`; normalize event types to lowercase snake case and reject unknown providers or missing fields with `ValueError`. Do not edit samples or tests. Run `rtk proxy python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v`, then report changed paths and the exact result.

cases.prompt: Build the v3 service-policy migration used by a deployment controller. The disjoint read-only partitions are `auth-manifest`, `billing-manifest`, `search-manifest`, and `worker-manifest`, each owning its matching file under `manifests/`. When the exact `spark_worker` role is available, delegate manifest inspection before the parent reads those files. Every brief begins `partition_ids: <actual IDs>` and asks for service, source version, exact old field paths, and values with provenance. Use the fewest useful workers; one may own multiple partitions and no fixed cap applies. The parent alone edits `src/migrate_policy.py`, writes `migration_report.json`, integrates evidence once, and runs acceptance. `migrate_policy` must accept v1 and v2 documents and emit exactly schema version 3 with `service`, `execution.timeout_seconds`, `execution.max_attempts`, and `access.enabled`; reject unsupported versions or malformed values with `ValueError`. Do not edit manifests or tests. Run `rtk proxy python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v`, then report changed paths and the exact result.

cases.prompt: Complete the inventory incident fix from the supplied on-call handoff. Work locally and do not delegate: the incident rules, event order, implementation, and report are tightly coupled. Read `handoff/incident.md` and `data/events.jsonl`, implement `summarize_inventory` in `src/inventory.py`, and write `incident_summary.json`. Duplicate event IDs must be ignored, shipments consume both on-hand and reserved units, releases only reduce reserved units, and no intermediate count may become negative. The summary must be deterministic by SKU and include on-hand, reserved, available, and duplicate-event count. Reject invalid event sequences with `ValueError`. Do not edit the handoff, events, or tests. Run `rtk proxy python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v`, then report changed paths and the exact result.

seed_files.handoff/incident.md: Process events in file order. Duplicate IDs are audit noise and never mutate state. receive adds on_hand; reserve adds reserved; release subtracts reserved; ship subtracts both on_hand and reserved. available is on_hand minus reserved. Any negative intermediate value is invalid.

cases.prompt: Fix the support-ticket CSV export safety defect. Work locally and do not delegate because schema interpretation, sanitization, output generation, and audit accounting are tightly coupled. Read `schema.json` and `data/tickets.json`, implement `safe_csv_rows` in `src/exporter.py`, and write both `tickets.csv` and `export_report.json`. Preserve column order from the schema and row order from the input. For text cells whose first non-whitespace character is `=`, `+`, `-`, or `@`, prefix the original cell with one apostrophe; do not alter integer fields or safe text. Use Python's CSV quoting rules. The report must contain row count, exact sanitized-cell count, and columns. Reject missing fields or wrong primitive types with `ValueError`. Do not edit inputs or tests. Run `rtk proxy python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v`, then report changed paths and the exact result.

seed_files.tests/test_exporter.py:
import json
import unittest
from pathlib import Path
from src.exporter import safe_csv_rows

ROOT = Path(__file__).parents[1]

class ExportTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def inputs(self):
        return json.loads((ROOT/'schema.json').read_text()), json.loads((ROOT/'data/tickets.json').read_text())

    def test_exact_export_and_report(self):
        csv_text, report = safe_csv_rows(*self.inputs())
        expected = '''ticket_id,requester,subject,priority\r\n101,Ana,'=IMPORTXML(A1),2\r\n102,' +cmd,Login issue,1\r\n103,Mo,"'@SUM(1,1)",3\r\n'''
        self.assertEqual(csv_text, expected)
        self.assertEqual(report, {'rows':3,'sanitized_cells':3,'columns':['ticket_id','requester','subject','priority']})
        self.assertEqual((ROOT/'tickets.csv').read_text(newline=''), expected)
        self.assertEqual(json.loads((ROOT/'export_report.json').read_text()), report)

    def test_wrong_types_and_missing_fields_fail(self):
        schema, tickets = self.inputs()
        bad = [dict(tickets[0], priority='high')]
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError): safe_csv_rows(schema, bad)
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError): safe_csv_rows(schema, [{'ticket_id':1}])
